```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – stretch towards/away from the 5 wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% board size: rows and columns 0..9
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% the row that contains a 5 in every column (the “wall”)
wall(W) :- cell(W,0), #count { C : input(W, C, 5) } = 10.

% colour parity (colours 0..9 are known via color/1)
even(C) :- color(C), C mod 2 = 0.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C mod 2 = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Forced cells created by the stretch rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Even colour, source above the wall – fill downwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    R0 < W,
    R0 < R,
    R < W,
    R >= 0,
    R <= 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Even colour, source below the wall – fill upwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    R0 > W,
    R0 > R,
    R > W,
    R >= 0,
    R <= 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour, source above the wall – fill upwards away from the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    R0 < W,
    R < R0,
    R >= 0,
    R <= 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour, source below the wall – fill downwards away from the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    R0 > W,
    R > R0,
    R <= 9,
    R >= 0,
    not input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition – exactly one colour per cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Clr) : color(Clr) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints enforcing the transformation
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Original non‑zero, non‑5 cells keep their colour
:- input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0, Orig != 5, not output(R,C,Orig).

% 2. Wall cells stay colour 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != 5.

% 3. Forced cells must receive the forced colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% 4. A cell cannot receive two different forced colours
:- forced(R,C,Col1), forced(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% 5. Propagation must not land on a wall cell
:- forced(R,C,Col), input(R,C,5).

% 6. Cells that are neither forced nor an original non‑zero/non‑5 cell keep the input colour
:- not forced(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 5, not output(R,C,Orig).

% 7. At most one colour may be assigned to a cell (redundant safety)
:- output(R,C,Clr1), output(R,C,Clr2), Clr1 != Clr2, cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

Now the final program:

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – stretch towards/away from the 5 wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% board: rows and columns 0..9
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% wall: the row that has a 5 in every column
wall(W) :- cell(W,0), #count { C : input(W, C, 5) } = 10.

% colour parity (colours 0..9 are known via color/1)
even(C) :- color(C), C mod 2 = 0.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C mod 2 = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Forced cells produced by the stretch rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Even colour, source above the wall – fill downwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    R0 < W,
    R0 < R,
    R < W,
    R >= 0,
    R <= 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Even colour, source below the wall – fill upwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    R0 > W,
    R0 > R,
    R > W,
    R >= 0,
    R <= 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour, source above the wall – fill upwards away from the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    R0 < W,
    R < R0,
    R >= 0,
    R <= 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour, source below the wall – fill downwards away from the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    R0 > W,
    R > R0,
    R <= 9,
    R >= 0,
    not input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition – exactly one colour per cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Clr) : color(Clr) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints enforcing the transformation
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Original non‑zero, non‑5 cells keep their colour
:- input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0, Orig != 5, not output(R,C,Orig).

% 2. Wall cells stay colour 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != 5.

% 3. Forced cells must receive the forced colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% 4. Two different forced colours may not be assigned to the same cell
:- forced(R,C,Col1), forced(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% 5. Propagation must not land on a wall cell
:- forced(R,C,Col), input(R,C,5).

% 6. Cells that are neither forced nor an original non‑zero/non‑5 cell keep the input colour
:- not forced(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 5, not output(R,C,Orig).

% 7. At most one colour may be assigned to a cell (redundant safety)
:- output(R,C,Clr1), output(R,C,Clr2), Clr1 != Clr2, cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – stretch towards/away from the 5 wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% domain: all cells of the board (rows and columns 0..9)
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% wall row: the unique row that contains a 5 in every column
wall(W) :- cell(W,0), #count { C : input(W, C, 5) } = 10.

% colour parity (colours 0..9 are known via color/1)
even(C) :- color(C), C mod 2 = 0.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C mod 2 = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Forced cells created by the stretch rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Even colour, source above the wall – fill downwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, R0 < R, R < W, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).

% Even colour, source below the wall – fill upwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, R0 > R, R > W, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour, source above the wall – fill upwards away from the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, R < R0, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour, source below the wall – fill downwards away from the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, R > R0, R <= 9, R >= 0, not input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition – exactly one colour per cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Clr) : color(Clr) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints enforcing the transformation
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Original non‑zero, non‑5 cells keep their colour
:- input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0, Orig != 5, not output(R,C,Orig).

% 2. Wall cells stay colour 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != 5.

% 3. Forced cells must receive the forced colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% 4. Two different forced colours may not be assigned to the same cell
:- forced(R,C,Col1), forced(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% 5. Propagation must not land on a wall cell
:- forced(R,C,Col), input(R,C,5).

% 6. Cells that are neither forced nor an original non‑zero/non‑5 cell keep the input colour
:- not forced(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 5, not output(R,C,Orig).

% 7. At most one colour may be assigned to a cell (redundant safety)
:- output(R,C,Clr1), output(R,C,Clr2), Clr1 != Clr2, cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – stretch towards/away from the 5 wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% domain: all cells of the board (rows and columns 0..9)
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% wall row: the unique row that contains a 5 in every column
wall(W) :- cell(W,0), #count { C : input(W, C, 5) } = 10.

% colour parity (colours 0..9 are known via color/1)
even(C) :- color(C), C mod 2 = 0.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C mod 2 = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Forced cells created by the stretch rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Even colour, source above the wall – fill downwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, R0 < R, R < W, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).

% Even colour, source below the wall – fill upwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, R0 > R, R > W, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour, source above the wall – fill upwards away from the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, R < R0, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour, source below the wall – fill downwards away from the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, R > R0, R <= 9, R >= 0, not input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition – exactly one colour per cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Clr) : color(Clr) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints enforcing the transformation
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Original non‑zero, non‑5 cells keep their colour
:- input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0, Orig != 5, not output(R,C,Orig).

% 2. Wall cells stay colour 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != 5.

% 3. Forced cells must receive the forced colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% 4. Two different forced colours may not be assigned to the same cell
:- forced(R,C,Col1), forced(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% 5. Propagation must not land on a wall cell
:- forced(R,C,Col), input(R,C,5).

% 6. Cells that are neither forced nor have a forced colour keep the input colour
:- input(R,C,Orig), not forced(R,C,_), not output(R,C,Orig).

% 7. At most one colour may be assigned to a cell (redundant safety)
:- output(R,C,Clr1), output(R,C,Clr2), Clr1 != Clr2, cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
even(C) :- color(C), C mod 2 = 0.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C mod 2 = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Even colour, source above the wall – fill downwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, R0 < R, R < W, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).

% Even colour, source below the wall – fill upwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, R0 > R, R > W, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall” rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  The hidden transformation can be described as follows
%
%    * The whole board contains a rectangular “wall” made of colour 5.
%      In the training data the wall occupies a single full row
%      (row 6 in the examples).  The row that contains a 5 in
%      *every* column is taken as the centre row C.
%
%    * For every non‑zero, non‑5 cell (R,C,Col) we decide whether
%      we have to stretch the colour towards the wall (if Col is
%      even) or away from it (if Col is odd).
%
%    * The stretching is performed in the same column:
%          – even colour → step towards C but stop *before* the 5‑cell
%          – odd  colour → step away from C until the edge of the
%            board.
%      All cells on the generated line obtain the original colour.
%
%    * The original cell itself keeps its colour.
%
%    * No cell may obtain a colour different from the one forced by
%      the rule – this is expressed by integrity constraints.
%
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Auxiliary predicates
% --------------------------------------------------------------

col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

row_five_count(R,N) :-
    aggregate(N = #count { input(R,_,5) }, N).

greater(N,M) :- N > M.

row_center(C) :-
    row_five_count(C,N),
    max_five_count(M),
    N = M.

max_row(M) :-
    input(R,_,_),
    M = R,
    not greater(M,R2).

above_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R < C.

below_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R > C.

even(C) :- color(C), C \ 2 = 0.

odd(C)  :- color(C), C \ 2 = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Domain – all cells that appear in the input
% --------------------------------------------------------------

output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Choice – exactly one colour for every output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------

{ output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Integrity constraints – enforce the transformation rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. every original coloured cell (≠0,≠5) must keep its colour
:- not output(R,C,Col0),
   input(R,C,Col0),
   col(Col0),               % keep the column for readability
   Col0 != 0,
   Col0 != 5,
   not output(R,C,Col0).

% 2. propagation for *even* colours – towards the centre row
%    (stop one row before the first 5‑cell in that column)
:- even(Col0), input(R0,C0,Col0), above_center(R0),   % above the wall → go down
   CenterRow, row_center(CenterRow),                 % centre row variable
   R1 = R0 + 1, R1 < CenterRow, not input(R1,C0,5), output(R1,C0,Col0).

:- even(Col0), input(R0,C0,Col0), below_center(R0),   % below the wall → go up
   CenterRow, row_center(CenterRow),                 % centre row variable
   R1 = R0 - 1, R1 > CenterRow, not input(R1,C0,5), output(R1,C0,Col0).

% 3. continuation of the even‑colour stretch (above)
:- even(Col0), input(R0,C0,Col0), above_center(R0), CenterRow, row_center(CenterRow),
   R1 = R0 + 1, R1 < CenterRow, not input(R1,C0,5), output(R1,C0,Col0),
   R2 = R1 + 1, R2 < CenterRow, not input(R2,C0,5), output(R2,C0,Col0),
   R3 = R2 + 1, R3 < CenterRow, not input(R3,C0,5), output(R3,C0,Col0),
   R4 = R3 + 1, R4 < CenterRow, not input(R4,C0,5), output(R4,C0,Col0),
   R5 = R4 + 1, R5 < CenterRow, not input(R5,C0,5), output(R5,C0,Col0).

% 4. continuation of the even‑colour stretch (below)
:- even(Col0), input(R0,C0,Col0), below_center(R0), CenterRow, row_center(CenterRow),
   R1 = R0 - 1, R1 > CenterRow, not input(R1,C0,5), output(R1,C0,Col0),
   R2 = R1 - 1, R2 > CenterRow, not input(R2,C0,5), output(R2,C0,Col0),
   R3 = R2 - 1, R3 > CenterRow, not input(R3,C0,5), output(R3,C0,Col0),
   R4 = R3 - 1, R4 > CenterRow, not input(R4,C0,5), output(R4,C0,Col0).

% 5. propagation for *odd* colours – away from the centre row
%    (stop at the board edge)
:- odd(Col0), input(R0,C0,Col0), above_center(R0), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 1, not input(R1,C0,5), output(R1,C0,Col0).

:- odd(Col0), input(R0,C0,Col0), below_center(R0), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= max_row(M), not input(R1,C0,5), output(R1,C0,Col0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program
% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the wall row (the row that is completely colour 5)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row_five_count(R,N) :-
    aggregate(N = #count { C : input(R,C,5) }).
wall_row(W) :-
    row_five_count(W,10).        % 10 columns in the board

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour parity
% --------------------------------------------------------------
even(C) :- color(C), C mod 2 = 0.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C mod 2 = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Propagation (the “stretch” rule)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Even colour – stretch towards the wall
propagate(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), even(Col),
    R0 < W, R0 < R, R < W,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Even colour – stretch towards the wall (source below the wall)
propagate(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), even(Col),
    R0 > W, W < R, R < R0,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour – stretch away from the wall (source above the wall)
propagate(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col),
    R0 < W, R >= 0, R < R0,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour – stretch away from the wall (source below the wall)
propagate(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col),
    R0 > W, R > R0, R <= 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Helper predicate: does a cell receive any propagation?
% --------------------------------------------------------------
prop_any(R,C) :- propagate(R,C,_).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :- propagate(R,C,Col).            % cells that are stretched

output(R,C,Col0) :- input(R,C,Col0), not prop_any(R,C). % untouched cells keep original colour

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints – enforce the correct transformation
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. No propagation onto a wall cell
:- propagate(R,C,Col), input(R,C,5).

% 2. Propagation must not overwrite a different original non‑zero, non‑5 colour
:- propagate(R,C,Col), input(R,C,Orig),
    Orig != 0, Orig != 5, Orig != Col.

% 3. Two different colours cannot be propagated to the same cell
:- propagate(R,C,Col1), propagate(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% 4. Exactly one colour per cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).
1 { output(R,C,Clr) : color(Clr) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
wall(R) :- #count { C : input(R, C, 5) } = 10.

% Parity of colours (colours 0..9 are hard‑coded via color/1)
even(C) :- color(C), C mod 2 = 0.

% Forced cells produced by the stretch rule

% Even colours, source above the wall: fill all cells between source and wall

    input(R0,C,c), even(c),
    wall(RW), R0 < RW,
    R0 < R, R < RW,
    R = 0..9,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Even colours, source below the wall: fill only the cell directly above the source

    wall(RW), R0 > RW,
    R = R0 - 1,
    R > RW,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colours, source above the wall: fill all cells above the source

    input(R0,C,c), odd(c),
    wall(RW), R0 < RW,
    R = 0..R0-1,
    not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colours, source below the wall: fill all cells below the source

    wall(RW), R0 > RW,
    R = R0+1..9,

:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,c), c != 5.

% No two different colours may be assigned to the same cell.
:- output(R,C,c1), output(R,C,c2), c1 != c2.

:- output(R,C,c), input(R,C,5), c != 5.
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – stretch towards/away from the 5 wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% domain: all cells of the board (rows and columns 0..9)
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% wall row: the unique row that contains a 5 in every column
wall(W) :- cell(W,0), #count { C : input(W, C, 5) } = 10.

% colour parity (colours 0..9 are known via color/1)
even(C) :- color(C), C mod 2 = 0.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C mod 2 = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Forced cells created by the stretch rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Even colour, source above the wall – fill downwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, R0 < R, R < W, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).

% Even colour, source below the wall – fill upwards towards the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, R0 > R, R > W, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour, source above the wall – fill upwards away from the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, R < R0, R >= 0, R <= 9, not input(R,C,5).

% Odd colour, source below the wall – fill downwards away from the wall
forced(R,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, R > R0, R <= 9, R >= 0, not input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition – exactly one colour per cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Clr) : color(Clr) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints enforcing the transformation
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Original non‑zero, non‑5 cells keep their colour
:- input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0, Orig != 5, not output(R,C,Orig).

% 2. Wall cells stay colour 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != 5.

% 3. Forced cells must receive the forced colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% 4. Two different forced colours may not be assigned to the same cell
:- forced(R,C,Col1), forced(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% 5. Propagation must not land on a wall cell
:- forced(R,C,Col), input(R,C,5).

% 6. Cells that are neither forced nor have a forced colour keep the input colour
:- input(R,C,Orig), not forced(R,C,ColTmp), not output(R,C,Orig).

% 7. At most one colour may be assigned to a cell (redundant safety)
:- output(R,C,Clr1), output(R,C,Clr2), Clr1 != Clr2, cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% Define the wall row: the unique row that has a 5 in every column
wall(W) :- #count { C : input(W, C, 5) } = 10.

% Parity of colours (excluding the wall colour 5)
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 2.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 4.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 6.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 8.

odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 1.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 3.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 5.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 7.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 9.

% Auxiliary predicate for the exact set of cells
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per cell (choose from allowed colours)
1 { output(R,C,Clr) : color(Clr) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Forced cells produced by the stretch rule
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

% Even colour, source above the wall (downwards towards the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 < W,
    R0 < R,
    R < W.

% Even colour, source below the wall (upwards towards the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 > W,
    R0 > R,
    R > W.

% Odd colour, source above the wall (upwards away from the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 < W,
    R < R0.

% Odd colour, source below the wall (downwards away from the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 > W,
    R > R0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraints enforcing the transformation
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

% Wall cells must stay colour 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != 5.

% Forced cells must appear with the forced colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Forced cells cannot have any other colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != Col.

% Cells that are not forced keep their original colour (except wall colour 5)
:- input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 5, not forced(R,C,_), not output(R,C,Orig).
```

```asp
% Determine the wall row (the unique row with a 5 in every column)
wall(W) :- #count { C : input(W, C, 5) } = 10.

% Parity of colours (exclude the wall colour 5)
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 2.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 4.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 6.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 8.

odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 1.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 3.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 5.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 7.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 9.

% All cells of the board
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per cell (choose from allowed colours)
1 { output(R,C,Clr) : color(Clr) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Forced cells generated by the stretch rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Even colour – source above the wall (downwards)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 < W,
    R0 < R,
    R < W.

% Even colour – source below the wall (upwards)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 > W,
    R0 > R,
    R > W.

% Odd colour – source above the wall (upwards away from the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 < W,
    R < R0.

% Odd colour – source below the wall (downwards away from the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 > W,
    R > R0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraints enforcing the transformation
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Wall cells stay colour 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != 5.

% Forced cells must be assigned the forced colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Forced cells cannot have any other colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != Col.

% Cells that are not forced keep their original colour (except the wall colour 5)
:- input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 5, not forced(R,C,_), not output(R,C,Orig).
```

refinements.response:
```asp
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine the unique row that consists entirely of colour 5 (the wall)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall(W) :- #count { C : input(W, C, 5) } = 10.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Colour parity (wall colour 5 is never used as a source)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 2.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 4.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 6.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 8.

odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 1.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 3.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 5.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 7.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 9.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% All board cells (input contains every cell)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per cell (choose from the allowed colours 0..9)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Clr) : color(Clr) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Forced cells created by the stretch rule
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Even colour – source above the wall (downwards towards the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 < W,
    R0 < R,
    R < W.

% Even colour – source below the wall (upwards towards the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 > W,
    R0 > R,
    R > W.

% Odd colour – source above the wall (upwards away from the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 < W,
    R < R0.

% Odd colour – source below the wall (downwards away from the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 > W,
    R > R0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraints enforcing the transformation
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

% Wall cells must stay colour 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != 5.

% Forced cells must be assigned their forced colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Forced cells cannot have any other colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != Col.

% Cells that are not forced keep their original colour (except the wall colour 5)
:- input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 5, not forced(R,C,X), not output(R,C,Orig).
```

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine the unique row that consists entirely of colour 5 (the wall)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall(W) :- #count { C : input(W, C, 5) } = 10.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Colour parity (wall colour 5 is never used as a source)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 2.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 4.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 6.
even(Col) :- color(Col), Col = 8.

odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 1.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 3.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 5.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 7.
odd(Col)  :- color(Col), Col = 9.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% All board cells (input contains every cell)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per cell (choose from the allowed colours 0..9)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Clr) : color(Clr) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Forced cells created by the stretch rule
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Even colour – source above the wall (downwards towards the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 < W,
    R0 < R,
    R < W.

% Even colour – source below the wall (upwards towards the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 > W,
    R0 > R,
    R > W.

% Odd colour – source above the wall (upwards away from the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 < W,
    R < R0.

% Odd colour – source below the wall (downwards away from the wall)
forced(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    wall(W),
    input(R,C,_),
    not input(R,C,5),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    R0 > W,
    R > R0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraints enforcing the transformation
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

% Wall cells must stay colour 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != 5.

% Forced cells must be assigned their forced colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Forced cells cannot have any other colour
:- forced(R,C,Col), output(R,C,Clr), Clr != Col.

% Cells that are not forced keep their original colour (except the wall colour 5)
:- input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 5, not forced(R,C,X), not output(R,C,Orig).
